# KVTX Script Vectors

These vectors characterize KVTXScript framing before KMP extraction. They use raw script bytes and expected extracted values so shared code can preserve write, append, remove, missing-key, unknown-command, and little-endian integer behavior without relying on platform-specific test construction. `KvtxCommonPolicyTest.kt` now executes shared write-and-commit framing, append/remove extraction, EX zero-index and non-empty-index behavior, unsigned key handling, unknown-command stop behavior, typed malformed-script rejection, and `kvtx-readiness.json` behavior-family coverage before production KVTX code moves.

`kvtx-readiness.json` names the pre-migration behavior-family gate for KVTX script migration: write-and-commit framing, empty-data write framing, unsigned 32-bit key preservation, multiple-key selection, append concatenation, remove clearing, extended write/append/remove zero-index behavior, non-empty extended-index ignore policy, unknown-command stop policy, malformed-script typed error policy, truncated-payload platform vector references, platform KVTX vector references, and the `compile-verification-gate` must remain covered before KVTX script code moves into shared KMP.
